name: Clam Chowder #2
recipeIngredient: 5 small Can Minced Clams OR 1 large Can (about 51-oz)
2 tsp Chesapeake Brand Bay-style Seafood Seasoning
recipeInstructions:
1. The day before serving, fry the bacon until crisp and remove it from the oil.
2. In the bacon drippings, sauté onion, celery with leaves, and potatoes for 10-15 minutes over medium heat.
3. Add the minced clams (not clam soup) to the mixture along with their juice.
4. Crumble the bacon into the pot and stir.
5. Cover the soup and simmer on low heat, stirring occasionally, for several hours – up to five hours is acceptable.
6. Allow the soup to cool completely and refrigerate overnight.
7. On the day of serving, skim off any grease from the top.
8. Transfer the soup to a larger pot.
9. Heat the soup until it begins to bubble and add the seafood seasoning.
10. Mix 2 cups of half and half with flour in a separate bowl.
11. Add this mixture to the chowder and stir until it bubbles again.
12. Stir in the remaining half and half and simmer, stirring constantly, until the desired thickness is reached.
13. Serve hot with garlic bread and a green salad.
